skill.name: codex-review
skill.description: Professional code review guidance with CHANGELOG update practices for Codex workflows.
skill.summary: Review code changes and prepare clear CHANGELOG updates in Codex.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, a hardcoded URL, and weak cryptography indicators. Review found these are documentation-only patterns: a fenced installation command, a GitHub reference, and substring matches in plain text. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, exfiltration, or executable skill code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 17. This line starts a fenced bash example for installation documentation and does not execute code inside the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 17 is a Markdown fence marker, not runtime code. The surrounding context is an installation section.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Close Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 19. This line closes the fenced bash block and has no command execution behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 19 is only a Markdown fence delimiter. It cannot execute commands by itself.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Link to GitHub
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL at SKILL.md line 27 points readers to a GitHub repository for examples. No automated network request, data transfer, or exfiltration path is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in Markdown prose as a reference link. There is no code that sends data to the URL.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Substring in Metadata
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 appears to be a substring match in frontmatter text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, or security-sensitive code appears on this line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a description string. It contains no use of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or related cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Substring in Troubleshooting Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 34 appears to be a substring match in plain documentation. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, or security-sensitive implementation appears on this line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 34 is a prose reference to troubleshooting guides. It does not define or invoke cryptographic behavior.

content.value_statement: Code changes need consistent review and release notes before they reach users. This skill guides Codex through structured review and CHANGELOG update workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides professional review of code changes before commits.
Supports CHANGELOG update planning for project changes.
Helps review large refactoring work with structured attention.
Works with Codex CLI triggers and natural language requests.
Encourages conventional commit messages and root CHANGELOG files.
limitations: The skill provides review guidance, not an automated static analysis engine.
It does not include executable scripts or tests in this package.
It depends on the AI assistant and local repository context for accuracy.
External repository examples may change outside this skill package.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Review
use_cases.description: Review pending changes for defects, regressions, and missing release notes before committing.

use_cases.title: Refactor Validation
use_cases.description: Check broad refactoring work for behavior changes, incomplete migrations, and unclear documentation updates.

use_cases.title: Release Note Preparation
use_cases.description: Convert reviewed changes into clear CHANGELOG entries that match the project style.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use codex-review to review my current changes and identify the most important issues before I commit.

prompt_templates.title: Changelog Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare release notes from a small change set
prompt_templates.prompt: Use codex-review to inspect my changes and suggest a concise CHANGELOG entry for this update.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a large internal code change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use codex-review to evaluate this refactor for regressions, missed call sites, and incomplete tests.

prompt_templates.title: Release Readiness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before a release branch merge
prompt_templates.prompt: Use codex-review to perform a release readiness review, including risks, test gaps, and CHANGELOG recommendations.

output_examples.input: Review my staged changes before commit.
output: A prioritized list of review findings with severity and file references.
A short summary of residual risk and suggested verification steps.
A proposed CHANGELOG note when the change affects users.

output_examples.input: Check this refactor for missed updates.
output: Potential behavior changes introduced by the refactor.
Files or tests that need follow-up review.
Documentation and release note changes that should be considered.

best_practices: Run the skill with a clean understanding of staged and unstaged changes.
Keep CHANGELOG.md in the project root when release notes are expected.
Ask for severity, evidence, and verification steps in review prompts.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the review as a replacement for tests or human approval.
Do not request broad rewrites when only a focused review is needed.
Do not publish CHANGELOG entries without checking project release conventions.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps Codex review code changes and prepare CHANGELOG updates with a structured review workflow.

faq.question: Does it run code automatically?
faq.answer: No executable automation is included in this skill file. It documents an installation command and review workflow.

faq.question: Can it replace normal tests?
faq.answer: No. It should complement tests, linters, and human review.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it create CHANGELOG entries?
faq.answer: It guides the assistant to propose CHANGELOG updates based on reviewed changes.

faq.question: Is network access required?
faq.answer: The skill includes a GitHub documentation link, but no automated network request is present in the skill file.
